Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and low total CO₂, indicating possible metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), with low specific gravity. HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Findings are consistent with acute or chronic kidney dysfunction, possible nephrotic syndrome, and metabolic derangement. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.
